headline: About Ashley Jae Carranza
bio: Ashley is a writer, educator, and creator based in Las Vegas. She teaches both high school and college writing, including courses in the prison system. That work, along with her own life, shapes how she thinks about storytelling, voice, and transformation.
She is currently working toward her MFA in creative writing and completing her novel A Boy Named Doctor.
secondaryImageAlt: Ashley Jae Carranza reading beside a bookshelf

currentWork.heading: Writing from lived experience
currentWork.description: Ashley is currently completing A Boy Named Doctor, a coming-of-age novel set in the California desert following a young boy navigating loss, identity, and survival. Through raw experiences and shifting relationships, he begins to understand who he is and who he might become.

editingExperience: PopMeC Research Blog — Peer Reviewer for horror, popular culture, American film, and Stephen King studies
Helen: a literary magazine — Contributing Editor and Fiction Reader
DASH Literary Journal — Fiction Editor and Reader

speakingTopics.title: Storytelling, Voice, and Transformation
speakingTopics.description: How lived experience, teaching, and creative practice shape stories that speak honestly to identity and change.

speakingTopics.title: Teaching Writing Across Contexts
speakingTopics.description: Insights from teaching high school, college, and prison-system writing classrooms.

speakingTopics.title: Popular Culture, Horror, and Film Studies
